title: sambucus nigra subsp. canadensis
intro: Sambucus nigra subsp. canadensis, also known as American elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Adoxaceae family, native to North America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of American elderberry include reducing symptoms of colds and flu, alleviating arthritis and muscle pain, and supporting cardiovascular health. American elderberry can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in capsule or supplement form to harness its medicinal properties.
